prompt_md: You manage an **AI-driven engineering portfolio** with a **fixed quarterly budget** and several initiatives competing for it. One is an **"AI velocity" dashboard** that reports **2× lines of code generated** but shows **no change in cycle time, defect rate, or value**. Two other teams — mobile and web — are each **independently building the same setup**: assistant config, golden patterns, secure scanning of generated code, and review automation. Budget is tight. **Pick the best portfolio decision and the correct reason.**

decision_choices: Fully fund the AI velocity dashboard because 2× lines generated proves the teams are more productive, and cut the mobile and web work
Kill the vanity-metric dashboard and redirect that budget into a shared AI-workflow platform (golden patterns, secure scanning, review automation) both the mobile and web teams adopt
Fund every initiative equally so no team feels singled out, even if each gets too little to succeed
Let each team keep building its own AI workflow so they stay fully independent
reason_choices: Because lines generated is a vanity metric that hasn't moved real delivery, so killing it frees budget, and funding the shared AI workflow once stops paying twice and makes every current and future team faster, safer, and more consistent
Because the initiative that generates the most code is always the most productive regardless of cycle time or defects
Because spreading a fixed budget evenly across all initiatives maximises the chance each one succeeds
Because every team building its own AI workflow and security scanning is a sign of a healthy, independent portfolio
